title: Repository Repair, Release, and Collaboration Capstone · Independent Repository Trial
summary: Recover and ship a repository containing mixed work, a conflict, a mistaken commit, a remote update, and a release deadline. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a complete Git foundations evidence portfolio with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Capture repository boundaries, config origins, current operation, worktree/index state, graph, remotes, and recovery refs before repair.
Order preservation, reproduction, local repair, tests, history cleanup, and publication from least to most irreversible.
Coordinate rewritten history, conflicts, ownership, release changes, and recovery instructions with explicit affected refs and people.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab repository incident combines mixed work, divergent refs, one conflict, an incorrect public commit, and release v5.0. Preserve recovery refs, repair the least destructive layer, and verify graph plus tree.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for capture repository boundaries, config origins, current operation, worktree/index state, graph, remotes, and recovery refs before repair.

content.text: A lab repository incident combines mixed work, divergent refs, one conflict, an incorrect public commit, and release v5.0. Preserve recovery refs, repair the least destructive layer, and verify graph plus tree.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Repository Triage condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Git Reference — Controls porcelain and plumbing command behavior, configuration, revision syntax, hooks, workflows, and recovery.

hints: Name the Repository Triage precondition or invariant that the lab case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: A lab repository incident combines mixed work, divergent refs, one conflict, an incorrect public commit, and release v5.0. Preserve recovery refs, repair the least destructive layer, and verify graph plus tree.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: git status alone captures all state needed for repository recovery.

options.text: git status alone captures all state needed for repository recovery. This does not resolve commit to the mission model.

For commit to the mission model, apply Repository Triage without its stated preconditions, omit the a complete Git foundations evidence portfolio trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Repository Triage for a complete Git foundations evidence portfolio.
State the Repository Triage preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write the lab evidence note for Handoff Runbook: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A lab repository incident combines mixed work, divergent refs, one conflict, an incorrect public commit, and release v4.0. Preserve recovery refs, repair the least destructive layer, and verify graph plus tree. Competency probe: Publish an accessible repository runbook covering setup, workflow, gates, release, recovery, and escalation with tested commands.
steps.why: Retrieving Handoff Runbook through the concrete a complete Git foundations evidence portfolio case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Publish an accessible repository runbook covering setup, workflow, gates, release, recovery, and escalation with tested commands.
